name: Vivid Poly
explanation: A bright, punchy 8-voice analog-style polyphonic synthesizer built for cutting leads and forward brass stabs. Each voice runs two DCOs — a band-limited saw and a variable-width pulse with PWM — plus a Ring control that morphs in oscillator-sync and ring-modulation grit for an aggressive metallic top end. The mix feeds a snappy resonant 4-pole low-pass driven by a fast, punchy filter envelope, then a quick amplitude AR; chords ring with independent contours and pitch tracks the keyboard. Controls: Cutoff, Resonance, Env Amount, PWM, Ring, Attack, Release and Level. Vivid and aggressive rather than mellow.
